description: Stage 3 of the Corthography Press pipeline for partners — distribute already-rendered content to the configured destination. Two phases: evaluate publishes to test (autonomous); release publishes to prod and requires explicit approval at the FABER phase boundary. The release phase invokes the CLI with --env prod, which will pause at the API's own release gate (awaiting_approval); partners with auto_approve_prod=false must coordinate with their press-core liaison to complete the run after the FABER gate is approved.

evaluate.description: Publish rendered content for {asset} to the test destination and wait for it to complete.

steps.name: Publish to test
steps.description: Invoke the partner CLI to publish rendered content for {asset} to the test destination, blocking until terminal. Use this output to validate the test destination before approving the prod release phase.
steps.prompt: Use the corthography-press-publish skill to start a template-publish run for target {asset} with --env test --wait. After the run completes, report the run_id and final status, and summarize what was published (file count from status output if available). Suggest the partner inspect the test destination before approving the release phase.

release.description: After human approval, publish {asset} to the prod destination. The CLI will trigger the API's release gate; the run sits at awaiting_approval until corthography approve is called.

steps.name: Publish to prod
steps.description: Invoke the partner CLI to publish rendered content for {asset} to the prod destination. Pass --wait so the CLI returns once the run reaches a terminal state OR exits with code 2 when the API pauses at the release gate. Report the run_id and exit code; if exit code 2, instruct the partner to run /corthography-press-approve {run_id} (or coordinate with press-core if auto_approve_prod is false on their registry).
steps.prompt: Use the corthography-press-publish skill to start a template-publish run for target {asset} with --env prod --wait. After the CLI exits, report: (1) run_id, (2) exit code, (3) final status. Exit code 2 means the run is paused at the API's release gate — do NOT retry; instead instruct the partner to run /corthography-press-approve {run_id}, noting that auto_approve_prod=false on the partner registry means a press-core engineer must approve.

autonomy.description: Test publishes run autonomously. Production publishes pause at the FABER release boundary and again at the API's own release gate (server-enforced).
